elements.label: Storyteller
elements.description: The GM / Storyteller using the desktop app. Configures roll settings, triggers rolls, manages dyscrasias and chronicle data, and monitors the coterie via the Campaign viewer.

elements.label: GM Roll Config
elements.description: The settings the Storyteller configures before making a resonance roll. Temperament settings (dice count, highest/lowest, thresholds) and resonance weights (per-type likelihood sliders). Ephemeral — resets on app restart.

elements.label: Dice Engine
elements.description: Resonance-domain RNG backend (`shared/dice.rs`, `shared/resonance.rs`). Takes a GM Roll Config and produces raw roll results — temperament dice (d10 pool with advantage/disadvantage), weighted resonance type picks, and acute checks (9–10 on a d10). Distinct from `shared/v5/` (the V5 skill-check helper library — backend-only, no shipped UI consumer yet).

elements.label: Resonance Roll Result
elements.description: The complete outcome of a single resonance roll. Carries all temperament dice, the selected die, temperament tier, resonance type, acute check, and an optional attached dyscrasia. The main product of the Resonance Roller that flows outward to exports, the bridge writeback path (Roll20 sheets and Foundry actors), and the event bus.

elements.label: Roll History
elements.description: In-memory list of past resonance roll results for the current session. Displayed in the Resonance Roller UI. Resets when the app restarts.

elements.label: Dyscrasia Store
elements.description: SQLite database table holding all dyscrasia entries — built-in V5 sourcebook entries (re-seeded on every startup per ADR 0002) and user-created custom entries. Each entry has a resonance type, name, description, bonus, and custom flag.

elements.label: Chronicle Store
elements.description: SQLite tables (chronicles, nodes, edges) holding all user-authored game state — areas, characters, businesses, merits, influence, and typed relationships. No seeding; fully user-authored.

elements.label: Saved Characters Store
elements.description: SQLite `saved_characters` table holding canonical snapshots of bridge-imported characters. Each row carries a source attribution (Roll20 / Foundry), the canonical character payload, and timestamps. Persisted independently of live VTT state, so saved characters survive bridge disconnects and let the Compare flow surface drift between saved and live.

elements.label: Bridge Live Feed
elements.description: Live character data streaming from one or more VTT sessions, normalized into a source-agnostic `CanonicalCharacter` shape with shared fields (hunger, health, willpower, humanity, blood potency) plus a per-source `raw` blob. Cache key is `<source>:<source_id>`. The frontend store consumes this uniformly regardless of whether the source is Roll20 or Foundry. See ADR 0006.

elements.label: Bridge Writeback
elements.description: Outbound traffic from the desktop app into a connected VTT. Three families today: (1) `bridge_set_attribute(source, source_id, name, value)` — generic per-character writes; Roll20 maps to sheet attributes, Foundry maps to `actor.update` or item create/delete via the `actor.*` helper umbrella; (2) `game.roll_v5_pool` and `game.post_chat_as_actor` — Foundry-only `game.*` umbrella helpers (FHL Phase 2) that issue dice rolls into Foundry chat or post actor-attributed chat lines via the WoD5e roll API; (3) `bridge_refresh` — request fresh snapshots.

elements.label: Markdown Export
elements.description: Resonance roll results saved as timestamped Markdown files to ~/Documents/vtmtools/. Each file contains the full roll result, any attached dyscrasia, and an export timestamp.

elements.label: Tool Event Bus
elements.description: Svelte store that lets tools broadcast events to each other without direct coupling. Currently carries `resonance_result` events (temperament, resonance type, acute status, dyscrasia name). Designed as the decoupling layer for future cross-tool features.

elements.label: Tauri Event Bridge
elements.description: Event channel carrying real-time updates from the Rust backend to the Svelte frontend using Tauri's built-in event system. Emits `bridge://roll20/connected`, `bridge://roll20/disconnected`, `bridge://foundry/connected`, `bridge://foundry/disconnected`, and `bridge://characters-updated` (carrying the merged `Vec<CanonicalCharacter>` across all sources). Distinct from the Tool Event Bus — this is backend-to-frontend, not tool-to-tool.

elements.label: Roll20 WS Listener
elements.description: Plain WebSocket server on `ws://127.0.0.1:7423` that connects the Tauri desktop app to the Roll20 Chrome extension. At most one extension session at a time. Carries both inbound (Roll20 live feed) and outbound (Roll20 writeback) traffic. Preserves the pre-bridge wire protocol byte-for-byte so the existing extension keeps working.

elements.label: Foundry WSS Listener
elements.description: TLS WebSocket server on `wss://127.0.0.1:7424` that connects the Tauri desktop app to the Foundry `vtmtools-bridge` module. wss is non-optional — Foundry is commonly served over HTTPS and browsers block ws://localhost from HTTPS pages as mixed content. Self-signed `localhost` cert generated by `rcgen` on first launch and persisted in the app data dir; the GM accepts the cert warning once per browser. Carries the dot-namespaced wire protocol (`actor.*`, `game.*`) plus extended Hello (protocol_version, capabilities, world_id), `bridge.subscribe`/`bridge.unsubscribe`, and a structured error envelope (Plan 0 consolidation).

elements.label: Resonance Roller
elements.description: The main resonance rolling tool. Consumes GM Roll Config, drives the Dice Engine, displays results, and distributes them to exports, bridge writeback, and the event bus. Also queries the Dyscrasia Store on Acute results. Reads the merged bridge live feed for character selection (works against either source).

elements.label: Dyscrasia Manager
elements.description: Full CRUD interface for browsing, searching, filtering, adding, editing, and deleting dyscrasias. Also supports rolling a random dyscrasia by resonance type.

elements.label: Campaign Viewer
elements.description: Live VTT character stat display. Shows hunger, health, willpower, humanity, and blood potency for all bridge-connected characters in real time across both Roll20 and Foundry. Also hosts the Saved Characters surface — Save/Update/Delete actions persist canonical snapshots into the Saved Characters Store, and a built-in Compare modal diffs saved-vs-live (drift badge per card; per-path before/after plus specialty-list comparison in modal).

elements.label: Domains Manager
elements.description: Hierarchical chronicle tracker. Lets the Storyteller model a game's territories, institutions, characters, and relationships as a graph of typed nodes connected by typed edges.

elements.label: Foundry Test Tool
elements.description: Dev/QA surface for the Foundry `game.*` helpers (FHL Phase 2). Lets the operator pick a Foundry actor from the bridge live feed and exercise `game.roll_v5_pool` and `game.post_chat_as_actor` end-to-end. Not a player-facing tool; exists to smoke-test the helper umbrella while downstream consumers (#9 roll-source toggle, #11 hunger/remorse/contested) are still planned.

elements.label: Roll20 Game Session
elements.description: The Roll20 browser-based virtual tabletop where the actual VTM game is played when running on Roll20. Source of all live Roll20 character data. The Roll20 extension runs inside this page.

elements.label: Foundry World
elements.description: A Foundry VTT world running with the WoD5e (vtm5e) system installed. Source of all live Foundry actor data. Can be hosted anywhere (Forge, Molten, self-hosted); only the GM's browser session connects to vtmtools.

elements.label: Roll20 Extension
elements.description: Chrome extension injected into Roll20 pages. Reads character data from Roll20's Backbone/Firebase data layer, debounces per-attribute Firebase events into 200ms whole-character snapshots, and relays them to the desktop app over `ws://127.0.0.1:7423`. Also receives writeback commands.

elements.label: Foundry Bridge Module
elements.description: The `vtmtools-bridge` Foundry module installed in the GM's world. Initialization is gated on `game.user.isGM` — only the GM's browser ever connects. Hooks `updateActor` / `createActor` / `deleteActor`; pushes the full actor list on `ready` then forwards individual changes. Receives outbound `actor.*` (sheet edits) and `game.*` (rolls + chat) wire messages and dispatches to the WoD5e API. Currently at module version 0.3.0 (Phase 2 game.* helpers shipped).

connections.label: TemperamentConfig + ResonanceWeights
connections.description: GM dials in dice_count, take_highest, negligible/fleeting thresholds, and per-type SliderLevel positions via the Resonance Roller settings panel.

connections.label: click → invoke('roll_resonance', RollConfig)
connections.description: GM clicks Roll. Component calls the typed wrapper, which invokes the Tauri command.

connections.label: CRUD on DyscrasiaEntry
connections.description: Add / edit / delete / search / random-roll-by-type via the Manager UI.

connections.label: Save / Update / Delete / Compare
connections.description: GM saves bridge-imported characters as canonical snapshots and opens the Compare modal to inspect saved-vs-live drift.

connections.label: CRUD on Chronicle / Node / Edge
connections.description: Author chronicles and the typed-graph nodes/edges that represent areas, characters, businesses, and relationships.

connections.label: fire test RollV5PoolInput / PostChatAsActorInput
connections.description: Dev surface; GM picks a Foundry actor and submits sample game.* envelopes to verify the helper umbrella end-to-end.

connections.label: RollConfig { temperament, weights }
connections.description: Frontend builds a RollConfig from the settings panel state and passes it to the Tauri invocation.

connections.label: invoke('roll_resonance', { config: RollConfig }) → ResonanceRollResult
connections.description: Single Tauri command — all randomness runs on the backend.

connections.label: ResonanceRollResult
connections.description: { temperament_dice: Vec<u8>, temperament_die: u8, temperament: Negligible|Fleeting|Intense, resonance_type?: enum, resonance_die?: u8, acute_die?: u8, is_acute: bool, dyscrasia?: DyscrasiaEntry }

connections.label: ResonanceRollResult → result card
connections.description: Component renders dice, temperament tier, resonance type, acute status, and (if attached) the dyscrasia.

connections.label: ResonanceRollResult appended
connections.description: In-memory list, reset on app restart.

connections.label: { type: 'resonance_result', temperament, resonance_type, is_acute, dyscrasia_name? }
connections.description: publishEvent() on the Svelte tool-events store — currently no subscribers, kept as the cross-tool decoupling seam.

connections.label: export_result_to_md(result) → ~/Documents/vtmtools/<timestamp>.md
connections.description: Tauri command writes a single timestamped file containing the full result + dyscrasia + export time.

connections.label: bridge_set_attribute(source, source_id, name='resonance', value)
connections.description: Generic per-character write; per-source semantics (Roll20: sheet attribute write; Foundry: WoD5e Item create after deleting prior resonance items).

connections.label: roll_random_dyscrasia(resonance_type) → DyscrasiaEntry
connections.description: Fired only when is_acute = true; selects randomly from built-in + custom rows of the matching resonance type.

connections.label: list/add/update/delete + roll_random_dyscrasia
connections.description: Five Tauri commands cover full CRUD + the random-by-type roll. DyscrasiaEntry { id, resonance_type, name, description, bonus, is_custom }.

connections.label: Chronicle / Node / Edge CRUD + tree queries
connections.description: 20 Tauri commands across chronicle.rs, node.rs, edge.rs. Includes derived tree queries (parent_of, children_of, path_to_root, subtree) over the 'contains' edge type.

connections.label: SavedCharacter { id, source, source_id, payload: BridgeCharacter, created_at, updated_at }
connections.description: Four Tauri commands (save/list/update/delete) on the saved_characters SQLite table. payload is the canonical bridge shape captured at save time; the Compare modal diffs it against the current Bridge Live Feed.

connections.label: trigger_foundry_roll(RollV5PoolInput) | post_foundry_chat(PostChatAsActorInput)
connections.description: Two Tauri commands behind src/lib/foundry-chat/api.ts. RollV5PoolInput { actor_id, value_paths, selectors?, difficulty?, label? }; PostChatAsActorInput { actor_id, content, roll_mode? }.

connections.label: Backbone models + per-attribute Firebase events
connections.description: Extension content script reads Roll20's internal Backbone data layer and listens to Firebase change events on each attribute.

connections.label: InboundMsg::Characters | InboundMsg::CharacterUpdate (whole-character)
connections.description: Per-attribute events are debounced 200ms into a single whole-character JSON: { id, name, controlled_by, attributes: [{ name, current, max }, …] }.

connections.label: → CanonicalCharacter (translated)
connections.description: Roll20Source::handle_inbound picks fields per docs/reference/roll20-bundle.md and writes a CanonicalCharacter into the merged cache keyed by 'roll20:<id>'.

connections.label: { type: 'set_attribute' | 'refresh', … }
connections.description: Outbound JSON frame built by Roll20Source::build_set_attribute / build_refresh.

connections.label: outbound JSON frame
connections.description: Same wire shape the extension sends back; preserves the pre-bridge protocol byte-for-byte.

connections.label: Backbone setAttribute(name, value)
connections.description: Extension translates the wire frame into Roll20 Backbone setter calls; creates the attribute if missing.

connections.label: game.actors + updateActor / createActor / deleteActor hooks
connections.description: Module reads the actor list on 'ready' and forwards subsequent change events; runs in GM browser only (gated on game.user.isGM).

connections.label: FoundryInbound::Hello | Actors | ActorUpdate
connections.description: Hello carries protocol_version + capabilities + world_id (Plan 0 consolidation). Actors / ActorUpdate carry FoundryActor { id, name, owner, system: actor.system blob }. Frame uses TLS over wss://localhost:7424.

connections.label: → CanonicalCharacter (translated from FoundryActor)
connections.description: FoundrySource::translate picks fields per docs/reference/foundry-vtm5e-paths.md (vampire/werewolf/hunter splat fields side-by-side). Cache key 'foundry:<id>'.

connections.label: actor.* / game.* / refresh envelopes
connections.description: actor.update_field, actor.create_feature, actor.create_item_simple, actor.append_private_notes_line, actor.delete_items_by_prefix, actor.delete_item_by_id, actor.apply_dyscrasia, game.roll_v5_pool, game.post_chat_as_actor — each owns a typed Rust builder in bridge/foundry/actions/<umbrella>.rs.

connections.label: dot-namespaced JSON frame { type: '<umbrella>.<verb>', … }
connections.description: bridge.js handler-map dispatch routes by msg.type prefix into foundry-actions/<umbrella>.js executors.

connections.label: actor.update / createEmbeddedDocuments / ChatMessage.create / WOD5E.api.RollFromDataset
connections.description: Executors call the Foundry / WoD5e APIs directly; V5 dice mechanics (hunger, messy crit, rouse, bestial fail) delegated to WoD5e's roll machinery.

connections.label: emit('bridge://characters-updated', Vec<CanonicalCharacter>)
connections.description: Whole merged cache across all sources is pushed as the event payload — frontend re-renders from the full list, never from a diff.

connections.label: bridge://characters-updated → bridge.svelte.ts store
connections.description: Component subscribes to the typed bridge store (never listen() directly) and re-renders cards for all connected characters.

connections.label: bridge://characters-updated → character picker
connections.description: Roller reads the merged store to populate the writeback target dropdown — works against any source.

connections.label: bridge://characters-updated → filter(source === 'foundry')
connections.description: Test tool restricts the picker to Foundry actors because game.* helpers are Foundry-only.
